rationale: The cited 2011 paper examines how the presence of a friend changes consumer spending, which matches the citing paper’s stated gap that prior marketing research documented behavioral social-context effects but not neural mechanisms. The citing study explicitly positions itself as extending such social-context work to EEG/ERP responses, so this citation is plausibly foundational rather than incidental. Even though the raw match is only a DOI with no local quote, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
